prompt:
Our config rewriter is mangling files and I had to restore two of them from backup this morning. It's `hostswap.ps1`, the little tool the rename runbooks call. Yesterday's run:

pwsh -NoProfile -File hostswap.ps1 -Path pool.conf -From db.pool.size -To db.pool.max

pool.conf before:

db.pool.size=25
# legacy alias, still read by the v1 agent
db_pool_size=25
db.pool.warm=5

pool.conf after:

db.pool.max=25 # legacy alias, still read by the v1 agent db.pool.max=25 db.pool.warm=5

Three problems in one run. The whole file is now ONE line. The legacy `db_pool_size` alias got renamed even though it is a different key — the v1 agent still reads it and this broke a canary box. And the tool printed `hostswap: 2 replacements in pool.conf` when there is exactly one `db.pool.size` in that file. Same story on an endpoints file: line breaks gone, and the staging host `metrics-host.example` got rewritten during a `metrics.host.example` swap — that's a different machine entirely.

The contract is simple: the file comes back byte-identical except that exact occurrences of the -From string become the -To string, and the reported count matches what actually occurred. -From and -To are literal strings from a runbook table, nothing fancy. It behaved fine in the quick check we did when it was first written, which is why it made it into the runbooks.

Fix hostswap.ps1 and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_hostswap.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P2 item 6: -Raw and join surprises, two related causes in one file (Read-ConfigText and the count/replace block of hostswap.ps1). Cause 1: Get-Content returns a line ARRAY and the innocent-looking "$content" normalization space-joins it, so every multi-line file collapses to one line and trailing-newline information is destroyed (the one-line fixture WITH trailing newline pins that too). Cause 2: [regex]::Matches and -replace treat the -From literal as a regex, so its dots match any character — db_pool_size and metrics-host.example get clobbered and the count inflates. Fix: Get-Content -Raw + [regex]::Escape on the pattern (probed 2026-07-16: 'db_pool_size=8' -replace 'db.pool.size' really does rewrite; escaped form does not). Both buggy lines look innocent in isolation; the prompt pastes the corrupted output and never names Get-Content, -Raw, regex, or escaping. Symptom is deterministic, no timing. Differentiation: first PS debug seed in the wave-6 strings chunk; nearest siblings are wave-4 sed/awk text-rewrite debug seeds (bash-sedbatch) whose causes are quoting, not object-model array-vs-string collapse — the array interpolation trap has no bash analog.
